explanation: The input requirement specifies a sequence: first, the self-test must complete successfully (i.e., selfTestPass), and the configurations must be loaded properly (i.e., initDone). Only after both of these conditions are true, the system is allowed to start monitoring and reporting health parameters (represented by startMonitoring and startReportingHealthParams). Therefore, the property is best captured as 'only after (selfTestPass & initDone), eventually satisfy (startMonitoring & startReportingHealthParams).' This expresses that monitoring and reporting health parameters can only start once both preconditions are met.
decision1_substring: Once the self-test has been completed successfully and configurations have been loaded properly
decision3_substring: the system shall start monitoring and reporting health parameters.
output_structured_natural_language: only after selfTestPass & initDone, eventually satisfy startMonitoring & startReportingHealthParams

From the atomic propositions, 'selfTestPass' captures the successful self-test and 'initDone' indicates that configurations are loaded. Thus, the condition 'selfTestPass & initDone' must be true before the activity 'startMonitoring & startReportingHealthParams' can start. We translate this as: Before 'selfTestPass & initDone', do not start monitoring/reporting. Once 'selfTestPass & initDone' holds, the system should start and always be reporting/monitoring health parameters. Therefore, the structured NL property specifies that after 'selfTestPass & initDone', the system should always satisfy 'startMonitoring & startReportingHealthParams'.

This directly models the requirement: the system may only eventually start monitoring and reporting parameters after both the self-test passed and the configuration loading is finished.
